title: Slapstick death animation — launch-up + spin-out tween before despawn
intent: Replace the instant despawn that fires when a bomber takes a lethal blast hit with a short ~0.5 s slapstick death tween: the bomber pops upward ~1.2 units, rotates around its forward axis a couple of turns, then falls and despawns. Round resolution should NOT wait for the animation — RoundResolveSystem still sees the bomber's `alive=false` flag the same frame it flipped, so the 3-second auto-restart timing is unchanged. The tween is pure presentation. This is the cheapest possible step toward the 'modern party game' tone (Boomerang Fu, Party Animals, Bombergrounds: Reborn all use slapstick death) and softens the competitive sting of a one-hit-kill arena without adding any new mechanic.
rationale: Source: GDD section 'Characters (planned MVP-3+)' notes slapstick death; pulling it down to MVP-1 because it costs almost nothing (one tween, no schema change) and the value is immediate. Reinforces the 'Local chaos, global clarity' pillar — the death moment is *more* readable when it's animated, not less. The S099 bomb-fuse-wiggle taming work has already established that AGF's Tween primitive is the right vehicle for this kind of presentation tweak. Slapstick death is also the lowest-risk lead-in to the procedural character generator in MVP-3: a rigged GLB humanoid that ragdolls is a 10x upgrade, but a primitive-capsule slapstick is a 1x stepping stone today.
acceptanceHints: examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/round-resolve-system.ts (or a sibling presentation system): when BomberStats.alive flips from true → false, schedule a Tween that lifts the bomber's Transform.position.y by +1.2 over 0.18 s ease-out, then drops it by ~1.5 over 0.32 s ease-in, while rotating Transform.rotation by 2π around Z.

Despawn (entity removal) happens at the END of the tween, not when alive flips. The bomber stays in the GridOccupancy index for the tween duration but is flagged non-blocking (other bombers + blasts pass through it).
RoundResolveSystem behaviour is unchanged: it still sees the alive=false flag the same frame and sets RoundState.phase. The 3 s auto-restart timing does not include the death tween.
Tween must not crash if the entity is force-despawned by scene reload before the tween completes (defensive — the round can restart while a death tween is mid-flight).
Audio: the existing `death` event still fires on the alive-flip frame; no new audio event is added by this story.
Visual: minimap marker disappears as soon as alive=false (matches the gameplay state, not the animation state). The 3D mesh is what slap-sticks.
Unit test: alive flips → entity stays present for tween-duration frames → entity removed afterwards.
Unit test: scene reload mid-tween leaves no leaked tween or entity reference.
